title: Accounts Merge (Union-Find)
problem_statement: Merge accounts with the same email address by connecting account entries that share one or more email addresses.

examples.explanation: Accounts are merged when they share any email address.

intuition: Treat emails as nodes and connect them by account; use union-find to group connected emails before formatting merged accounts.

approaches.name: Union-Find grouping
approaches.description: Map each email to a parent in a Union-Find structure and union emails in the same account, then collect groups by root.
approaches.time: O(A * α(N)) where A is total emails
approaches.why_not: DFS alone can work, but union-find simplifies grouping across many overlapping accounts.

interview_qa.q: What structure helps merge overlapping accounts?
interview_qa.a: Union-Find groups emails that share an account by connecting them via a common root.

interview_qa.q: Why sort emails in each merged account?
interview_qa.a: Sorting ensures stable output order and matches expected canonical representation.
